steps.title: Compare to the baseline
steps.content: The conclusion says nitrogen can more than offset climate-change damage under moderate change, but not under extreme change. To support that, the data must compare future nitrogen-treated growth with current-climate growth without nitrogen.

steps.title: Check the moderate case
steps.content: Under moderate change, growth with nitrogen is about 0.18 cm. Current-climate growth without nitrogen is about 0.16 cm. Since the moderate nitrogen value is higher, nitrogen more than offsets the moderate climate-change effect.

steps.title: Check the extreme case
steps.content: Under extreme change, growth with nitrogen is only about 0.06 cm. That is lower than the no-nitrogen current-climate baseline of about 0.16 cm, so nitrogen does not offset the damage when the climate change is extreme.

steps.title: Use both comparisons
steps.content:
• A: compares nitrogen-treated trees across scenarios but misses the no-nitrogen current baseline.
  • B: does not show moderate nitrogen beating the baseline.
  • C: stays within moderate-change and no-nitrogen comparisons.
  • D: shows moderate nitrogen above baseline and extreme nitrogen below baseline.
The answer is D.
